description:
Advances: none (test infrastructure only — no spec subject changes behavior)

Deliverable:
Root-cause fix for the slow merge gate that motivated specled_-s65.3. Investigation (session bbf3b419, 2026-07-02) found the tagged_tests verification command is slow because the test suite inherits the developer's global git config: commit.gpgsign=true + gpg.format=ssh + gpg.ssh.program=/Applications/1Password.app/Contents/MacOS/op-ssh-sign means every git commit the tests make in temp fixture repos does IPC with the 1Password app. Measured: 2.54s per signed commit vs 0.30s unsigned; full suite 366s with global config vs 174s with GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null (mix test --include integration, 816 tests, same host). The 59 verification_command_failed findings and the s65.3 timeout bump were symptoms of this leak, not of a slow host.

Fix: make the suite hermetic to host git config. In test/test_helper.exs, before ExUnit.start():
- System.put_env("GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL", "/dev/null")
- System.put_env("GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM", "1")
Env vars propagate to every child process the test BEAM spawns — all SpecLedEx.Case.git!/2 calls, the direct git show in branch_check_test.exs, lib code's read-only git calls during tests, and the child mix runs from SpecLedEx.IntegrationCase. No per-callsite changes needed: init_git_repo/1 already sets local user.name/user.email, so nothing from the global config is required. Add a short comment explaining why (host commit-signing config must not leak into fixture commits).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: full suite (mix test --include integration) green, 0 failures.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: suite wall time drops roughly in half on a host with 1Password commit-signing configured (~366s -> ~174s reference measurement); no test asserts on signed commits.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- test/test_helper.exs
- .spec/state.json (regenerated only, if spec.check rewrites it)

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**
- test_support/**
- test/** other than test_helper.exs
- .spec/specs/**
- .spec/config.yml

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not revisit the s65.3 command_timeout_ms value here (follow-up: with this fix the merged run is ~170s, so 400s becomes comfortable headroom; a later ticket may lower it to ~240s).
- Do not attempt the larger levers found in the same investigation (unglobalize Mix.shell to unlock async: false -> async: true on 33 files; double suite execution in the merge gate; excluding :integration from plain mix test) — candidates for separate tickets.
- No behavior change to shipped code.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test passes (full suite, --include integration)
- [ ] mix spec.check green within the configured timeout
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only test/test_helper.exs (+ regenerated .spec/state.json if applicable)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
WORKER_ERROR: mix spec.check cannot pass within ticket scope — test/test_helper.exs is in the specled.package `surface:` list, so touching it fires a strict branch_guard_missing_subject_update error; the only in-scope suppressant (Spec-Drift: test_only) downgrades it to :info but spec.check still fails on ANY finding.

Session: 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6
Worktree: /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-hermetic-git-config-tests
Branch: specled_-s65.4/hermetic-git-config-tests
Commit: 3280aae — "test: make suite hermetic to host git config" (test/test_helper.exs only, clean tree)

## The fix itself is done and verified
- test/test_helper.exs sets GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null and GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 before ExUnit.start(), with a comment explaining why.
- mix test --include integration: 816 tests, 0 failures; ExUnit finished in 70.0s (67.5s sync) — the 1Password signing IPC is gone.
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix format --check-formatted, mix deps.unlock --check-unused: all clean.

## Why the merge gate blocks (the spec gap)
1. .spec/specs/package.spec.md:22 lists `test/test_helper.exs` in the specled.package `surface:` block. A change to a surface file with no resolving realized_by binding fires branch_guard_missing_subject_update at strict :error (spec req specled.branch_guard.file_touch_surface_only_unbound_stays_strict). => mix spec.check exits 1.
2. Ticket Out-of-Scope forbids .spec/specs/** and .spec/config.yml, so the two "normal" fixes (co-change package.spec.md, or pin the severity in config.yml) are both off-limits.
3. Only in-scope lever = a Spec-Drift git trailer. The blessed preset `Spec-Drift: test_only` downgrades the finding to :info — but branch_check.ex:121 sets status via `if(findings == [], do: "pass", else: "fail")`, so ANY finding (incl. :info) fails the gate. VERIFIED: with test_only, spec.check still exits 1 with the finding at [INFO].
   - This contradicts specled's own scenarios file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades ("mix spec.check exits zero" for a lone :info finding) and file_touch_multi_subject_partial_attestation ("exits non-zero because of subj_b", i.e. the error finding). The unit test branch_check_test.exs:449 only asserts severity == "info", never status/exit — so "info exits zero" is specified but unimplemented. Looks like a real specled bug.

## Options for the orchestrator (each needs a scope decision I can't make as a leaf worker)
A. Add `Spec-Drift: branch_guard_missing_subject_update=off` to the commit trailer. VERIFIED green (findings=0, exit 0), spec-sanctioned (specled.branch_guard.file_touch_severity_config_wins says :off absorbs trailer overrides). Downside: branch-wide suppression of that code — would mask any legit missing_subject_update across the whole epic PR (right now only package fires; config's spec was co-changed by a prior stage).
B. Widen scope to allow a one-line .spec/specs/package.spec.md edit (drop test/test_helper.exs from `surface:`, or co-change it).
C. Fix the underlying specled bug (branch_check.ex status should not fail on :info-only findings), which makes the honest `test_only` trailer pass as the spec already promises. Touches lib/** — separate ticket.

Current state: implementation commit in place with the honest `Spec-Drift: test_only` trailer; spec.check exits 1 on the lone [INFO] finding. Not iterating further per instructions.

SCOPE_EXPANSION: spawned specled_-s65.8 to fix a genuine severity-blindness bug in branch_check.ex's status/exit computation (confirmed at lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex:121 + lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex:107 — contradicts branch_guard.spec.md's own scenario that an info-severity finding should let mix spec.check exit zero). Bouncing this ticket with the new ticket as a dependency. Does not consume a bounce attempt.

IMPLEMENTED

Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6.jsonl

Commit: 7b1196caf92d99a1ae16ac510a392e3af83ac6e9
Files touched: test/test_helper.exs (+10), .spec/state.json (regenerated by spec.check — allowed touch; now emits the realization key + BranchCheck/Realization index entries that the pre-rebase state.json lacked)
Tests added: none — test-infra-only change
Verification stubs flipped: none

Summary: Rebased the single hermetic-git-config commit cleanly onto the epic tip (5881d0d, which now carries s65.8's branch-check severity fix); no code-file conflicts, only .spec/state.json regen. The fix is unchanged — test_helper.exs sets GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null and GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1 before ExUnit.start() with an explanatory comment, and the commit carries the honest Spec-Drift: test_only trailer. Full merge gate now passes end-to-end: compile --warnings-as-errors clean, mix test --include integration 820 tests / 0 failures in 98.9s (vs the ~366s pre-fix baseline), format --check-formatted clean, deps.unlock --check-unused clean, and mix spec.check exits 0 (status=pass, errors=0, warnings=0; the missing_subject_update finding on test_helper.exs is now present at severity info rather than failing the gate, exactly as s65.8 promised). Working tree is clean and the state.json regen is idempotent.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: agent-averify-specled-s65-4-33979b6fc9c46c78 (parent 80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6)
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/80aca0d3-aeb1-4290-be1c-a32b31ea51b6/subagents/agent-averify-specled-s65-4-33979b6fc9c46c78.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (test/test_helper.exs, .spec/state.json)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0 (no spec subject — test-infra-only ticket), passed: N/A
- Requirements coverage: N/A (Advances = none); fix-correctness assessment instead:
  - Env vars set before ExUnit.start(): CONFIRMED. test_helper.exs lines 8-9 call System.put_env("GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL","/dev/null") and System.put_env("GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM","1"), with ExUnit.start() at line 11 after them. A 7-line comment explains the 1Password op-ssh-sign IPC cost and why nothing from global config is needed (init_git_repo/1 sets local user.name/email).
  - Propagation to child processes/git commits: CONFIRMED, no gap. git!/2 (test_support/specled_ex_case.ex:112, used by init_git_repo + commit_all) and the direct git show (branch_check_test.exs:424) both call System.cmd("git", ...) with NO env: option, so they inherit the parent BEAM env. The two IntegrationCase child-mix runs (specled_ex_integration_case.ex:36,78) pass only additive env: entries (MIX_ENV/ERL_LIBS/SPECLED_EX_EBIN) — System.cmd env: merges, does not clear — so GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/NOSYSTEM still propagate to them and their git subprocesses. grep confirms test_helper.exs is the ONLY place in lib/test/test_support that sets any GIT_CONFIG var; no call site clears or overrides it.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. .spec/config.yml untouched (verification.command_timeout_ms unchanged at 400s). No async: true conversion, no double-suite-execution change, no :integration exclusion. lib/** untouched — zero behavior change to shipped code.
- Project verification: PASS (all measured by me, not the implementer):
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
  - mix test --include integration: 2 properties, 820 tests, 0 failures. Wall clock 151.18s real (ExUnit "Finished in 148.0s"; 9.1s async / 138.8s sync) — vs the ~366s pre-fix baseline on this host. ~2.4x faster; the 1Password signing IPC is gone. (Slightly above the implementer's 98.9s, consistent with concurrent host contention during verification, still far under baseline.)
  - mix format --check-formatted: clean (exit 0)
  - mix deps.unlock --check-unused: clean (exit 0)
  - mix spec.check: EXIT 0, status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, findings=2 both at [INFO]. The required missing_subject_update finding is PRESENT at severity INFO on test/test_helper.exs (impacts specled.package) — not error, not absent. (Second INFO finding is on branch_check_test.exs from earlier epic stages; branch diff is vs origin/main = 16 files, not this commit.) Commit trailer Spec-Drift: test_only is the honest downgrade mechanism and is not suppressing any error-severity finding.
- Audit-expanded scope, if any: none.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Commit 7b1196c carries the honest Spec-Drift: test_only trailer; the info-severity gate pass is now legitimate because s65.8's branch-check severity fix (commit 19516e8) landed on the epic tip this stage rebased onto — spec.check no longer fails on info-only findings, exactly as branch_guard.spec.md's file_touch_attested_clean_downgrades scenario promises.
- Working tree is clean after my own spec.check/test runs, confirming the .spec/state.json regeneration is idempotent (pure regen, not hand edits).
- Advisory only (not a gate): the second [INFO] finding on branch_check_test.exs is inherited from prior epic stages and outside this ticket's commit; nothing to act on here.

title: Hermetic git config in test suite — stop 1Password commit-signing from doubling suite runtime
